mmBayes Bracket Workspace

Bracket entry workspace

Bracket year 2026. Start by copying a complete candidate bracket, then pause only at the marked review picks before opening deeper evidence.

UpdatedApril 24, 2026 at 05:09 PM EDT
Built from commit0b08e1f
Repo snapshotSynced to tracked repo output in this run
How to readEnter bracketReview picksTreeEvidenceReference
Status: Final result. First Four slots are resolved, so the displayed bracket path reflects finalized play-in outcomes.
Start here
How to read this workspace
Open this for the confidence legend, glossary, and row-reading guide.
Quick orientation

Read the signal first, then open evidence only when the call still matters.

The review queue tells you what deserves attention. The confidence styling tells you how stable the current recommendation is. The evidence drawers are for the handful of games where you still want the deeper matchup detail.

How to read a row
DotPosterior mean probability for the recommended side.
BarPosterior interval around that mean, showing how much uncertainty still remains.
ColorConfidence tier from Lock through Volatile so you can scan stability quickly.
Why the boards are ordered this way
Ranked boardSorted by decision score so the most consequential calls rise first.
Upset boardSorted by leverage so the highest-payoff underdog pivots are obvious.
Divergence mapShows where Candidate 2 splits from Candidate 1, with late-round route changes carrying more visual weight.
Confidence tiers
Lock
Lean
Toss-up
Volatile
Key terms

Short labels you will see on cards, tables, and evidence drawers.

BarthagOverall strength rating
AdjOEAdjusted offense
AdjDEAdjusted defense
WABWins above bubble
TORTurnover rate
TORDOpponent turnover rate
ORBOffensive rebounding
DRBDefensive rebounding
3P%Three-point make rate
3P%DOpponent three-point make rate
Adj T.Adjusted tempo
decision_scoreReview priority score
upset_leverageUpset payoff if it hits
confidence_tierLock / Lean / Toss-up / Volatile
inspection_levelPrimary, secondary, or watchlist

These are the short labels used in the dashboard cards and evidence panels. Seeds still appear beside every matchup.

Enter bracket

Candidate Recommendations

Use this section like a bracket sheet: choose Candidate 1 or Candidate 2, copy winners by region and round, then enter the champion and tiebreaker. Review markers show the picks worth pausing on.

Next action

Enter Candidate 1, then pause at the marked review picks.

Candidate 1 is the baseline bracket. Candidate 2 is the alternate if you want the changed route shown below.

Card roleBaseline reference

Illinois

Alternate entry
-24.797

Final Four: Duke, Arizona, Illinois, Michigan

Identity: Alternate bracket that keeps most early picks aligned but changes 6 late-round slots on the title route.

Path support: Observed in 1 of 50 coherent posterior-draw simulations.

Bracket log probability-24.797
Mean picked-game probability0.732
Championship tiebreaker152
Title-game total152.4 (132-172)
Changed slots from baseline15 total | 6 late-round

Pairwise diff: 15 total | 6 late-round relative to Candidate 1.

Projected title game: Illinois vs Duke

Review Queue

Use this after the fill-in view. Read the divergence route map to see where Candidate 2 actually splits, then work the queue for bracket-changing disagreements, upset pivots, and fragile favorites that deserve manual attention.

Confidence tiers
Lock
Stable favorite
Lean
Clear edge
Toss-up
Near coin flip
Volatile
Wide interval

Need more diagnostics?

The technical dashboard keeps ensemble validation, calibration, backtest, and component diagnostics separate from the bracket workflow.

Open technical_dashboard.html

Understand why

Bracket Tree

Each node is a game in the selected candidate's actual bracket path. Color shows confidence tier. Click any game to open its evidence drawer. Hover can add context, but the critical evidence is always available in the drawer.

LockLeanToss-upVolatileU Upset pick Candidate 2 route change
Understand why

Matchup Evidence

These are the targeted matchup drawers that the review queue actually surfaced. This is not a causal decomposition. Use the jump buttons in the review queue and tree to open a specific drawer.

Bracket-changing toss-upsSaint John's vs KansasRound of 32 | East | Seeds 5 vs 4

Bracket-changing toss-upsDuke vs Saint John'sSweet 16 | East | Seeds 1 vs 5

Why this surfaced: Mid-bracket fork. This is where the two paths start producing different title routes.

Downstream implication: Earlier picks already changed the path, so this bracket position is no longer the same game.
